summary: polipoli-pak postinstall exfiltrates host fingerprint and environment variable names to webhook.site
details: postinstall.js runs automatically on `npm install` and collects the installer's hostname, OS platform/arch, node version, current working directory, INIT_CWD, username, npm user-agent, and the full list of environment variable names, then POSTs that JSON payload to a hardcoded webhook.site endpoint (https://webhook.site/a428f027-90c9-45e2-acca-ffbb4ea86044) controlled by the package author. The behavior fires without consent on every install. A source comment self-labels the package as a bug-bounty proof-of-concept scoped to a specific vendor's build pipeline, but the postinstall executes against any installer of the public npm package, not only the labeled target; the self-label does not scope or gate the exfiltration.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
